---
title: Contributing
---

## Contributing to Essential Digital Skills

Contributions are welcome for:

- content corrections
- clearer explanations and examples
- improved teaching activities
- accessibility and usability improvements
- updated institutional references or broken links

## How to Contribute

1. Open an issue describing the change.
2. If editing content directly, use a focused branch and clear commit messages.
3. Submit a pull request with a concise summary of what changed and why.

## What Makes a Good Contribution

- Keep the material practical and actionable.
- Prefer concrete examples over abstract statements.
- Make local processes and responsibilities clearer, not vaguer.
- Flag policy or process references that may have changed.
- If a change affects delivery, note any implications for learners or instructors.

## Licensing and Attribution

By contributing to this course, you agree that your contribution can be published under the terms described in [LICENSE.qmd](LICENSE.qmd).

If you reuse material from another source, include enough information for that source to be credited and checked properly.

## Content Principles

- Keep material grounded in realistic research work.
- Prefer clear examples over abstract statements.
- Ensure references to policy and process remain current.

## Project Policies

- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.qmd)
- [License](LICENSE.qmd)
